name: referral-outreach-engine
description: Professional referral-network outreach the honest way: identify the referrer types that actually fit the practice, draft an introduction letter, a one-page practice summary, and a respectful follow-up cadence — then a human sends every message personally. No PHI, no invented relationships, no invented outcomes; the practice's real services and logistics are the only raw material.

steps.title: Referral-fit map: {audience}
steps.description: For the {project} practice, map which {audience} contacts are genuinely worth an introduction, based ONLY on the practice's real services, population, availability, and location. Describe the fit criteria and the categories of contact worth pursuing (for example: the kind of clinic, the kind of school role, the kind of employee-assistance coordinator). Do not invent a single named contact, a relationship, or an interest level — where a real list belongs, write [CHECK: clinician supplies actual names]. Output: fit criteria, contact categories, and what the practice honestly offers each.

steps.title: Introduction letter: {audience}
steps.description: Draft a short introduction letter from the clinician to a {audience} contact for the {project} practice. Plain, collegial, specific: who the clinician is, who they serve, what a smooth referral looks like, and how to reach the practice. Use [bracketed placeholders] for the recipient's name and any specifics the clinician personalizes per contact. No efficacy claims, no invented caseload facts, no urgency tricks — the letter earns a reply by being useful and honest about scope and availability.

steps.title: Practice one-pager: {project}
steps.description: Write the one-page practice summary a {audience} contact can keep on file: services, populations served, modalities offered, logistics (location, telehealth, hours, how to refer), and contact details — every line a verifiable practice fact from the {project} practice, [CHECK] where the clinician must confirm. No superlatives, no rankings, no outcome language. Format it so the practice can drop it into a document and print.

steps.title: Follow-up cadence: {audience}
steps.description: Propose a respectful follow-up rhythm for the {project} practice's {audience} outreach: when to follow up after the first letter, what a useful second touch contains (a new practice fact, an updated availability note — never pressure), and when to stop. Draft the short follow-up note templates with [bracketed placeholders]. The rule: every touch gives the recipient something true and useful, or it is not sent.

steps.title: Honesty review: {audience} outreach package
steps.description: Review the fit map, letter, one-pager, and cadence against the Shaula honesty rules. Confirm no invented contact, relationship, statistic, or outcome appears anywhere; every practice fact is real or marked [CHECK]; and the tone stays collegial, never salesy. Hold until clean.

steps.title: Human gate — clinician personalizes and sends: {audience} outreach
steps.description: HUMAN GATE: the clinician supplies the real contact names, personalizes each letter, and sends every message themselves from their own account. The office never contacts a referrer directly and never automates the sending. Record who was contacted and when, so the cadence step's follow-ups stay honest.
